summary: Executed targeted backend + frontend regression for exhibition workflow routes/contracts on public preview URL. Public exhibitions pages load, alias endpoints return valid payloads, protected artist/admin exhibition routes redirect without runtime crash, and artist pricing/payment-order endpoints enforce auth as expected.

integration_issues.issue: Console still reports failed request to https://checkout-static-next.razorpay.com/build/undefined (ERR_BLOCKED_BY_ORB) during exhibition page loads.

action_items: Investigate Razorpay script config/path causing /build/undefined fetch failures.
Run authenticated artist/admin create-flow retest (submission + payment paths) once credentials are available.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend endpoints /api/public/active-exhibitions and /api/public/archived-exhibitions are present and stable via alias handlers.
Artist pricing (/api/artist/exhibitions/pricing) and payment-order (/api/artist/exhibitions/payment-order) contracts are auth-protected and no longer return 500 on unauth access.
ArchivedExhibitionsPage interactive elements still have limited data-testid coverage, reducing automation robustness.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Authenticated end-to-end flows (artist exhibition submission, manual screenshot validation path, Razorpay completion callback) remain unverified due to missing credentials; only route health and unauth contracts were validated in this run.
rca of the issue: The remaining observable issue is third-party Razorpay static asset request to /build/undefined, indicating a likely checkout script/version bootstrap misconfiguration rather than route logic failure.
